meanings.definition: place into the hands or custody of
examples: Turn the files over to me, please
He turned over the prisoner to his lawyers

meanings.definition: cause to overturn from an upright or normal position
examples: The cat knocked over the flower vase
the clumsy customer turned over the vase

meanings.definition: move by turning over or rotating
examples: The child rolled down the hill
turn over on your left side

meanings.definition: turn up, loosen, or remove earth
examples: turn over the soil for aeration

meanings.definition: do business worth a certain amount of money
examples: The company turns over ten million dollars a year

meanings.definition: cause to move around a center so as to show another side of
examples: turn a page of a book

meanings.definition: turn from an upright or normal position
turn upside down, or throw so as to reverse

examples: They considered the possibility of a strike
Turn the proposal over in your mind
